title: boehmeria candicans
intro: Boehmeria candicans, also known as False Nettle or Polynesian Nettle, is a flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Boehmeria candicans can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
